basics.name: J. Miguel Sánchez Almagro
basics.label: Software Engineer · C++ / Embedded Software & Backend
basics.summary: Software engineer with ~3 years building and testing software in demanding, regulated domains — C++ embedded software (BMW automotive project), Java/Spring Boot backend (Capgemini), and automated testing of safety-critical systems (Airbus). I work on the software side: clean, maintainable, well-tested code (design patterns, refactoring, unit/integration testing). Strong CS foundation with an AI/Data-Science specialization and an MSc in Cybersecurity. I use LLMs and AI agents daily and am upskilling in AI engineering.

work.name: Bertrandt (client: BMW)
highlights: Developed, maintained and tested C++ embedded software for BMW's intelligent locking/unlocking feature, within an AUTOSAR-based project (application-software layer).
Refactored existing code by introducing design patterns — improving readability, efficiency, maintainability and testability.
Wrote unit tests (GoogleTest) and software-in-the-loop (SIL) integration tests in Python; Scrum with daily client feedback; CAN-based comms.

work.name: Bertrandt (client: Airbus)
highlights: Built and executed 1,000+ automated tests for the Airbus A400M cargo-section software using the client's proprietary C-based test framework.
Managed requirements and traceability in DOORS, to the DO-178 airborne-software standard.

work.name: Capgemini
highlights: Built and optimised Spring Boot services and REST APIs in Java over Oracle SQL for Adif's (Spanish rail) incident-reporting web app; owned the unit testing (JUnit); CI with Jenkins.
Briefly coordinated the team and reviewed code; Scrum via Jira.

courses: Thesis: C++/Linux digital-forensics tool for storage-device identification
Specialization in Artificial Intelligence / Data Science (ML, deep learning, computer vision, Big Data)

projects.name: Storage Device Forensics
projects.description: C++/Linux tool that gathers forensic identifiers from connected USB devices and disks (MSc thesis).

projects.name: AI Applied to Videogames
projects.description: Multi-agent game AI: two AI teams compete with no human input via a finite-state machine over steering, influence maps and pathfinding (team project).

projects.name: Computer Vision — OpenCV practicals
projects.description: Six computer-vision techniques: SIFT matching, chroma-key, camera calibration, augmented reality, image rectification.

meta.workRights: Working Holiday Visa (subclass 462) — full Australian working rights, based in Gold Coast, QLD. Available immediately for on-site (QLD or relocation) or remote work; open to 3–6 month contracts, project spikes, or contract-to-permanent roles.
